skill.name: signup-flow-cro
skill.description: Optimizes signup, registration, account creation, and trial activation flows by reducing friction and improving completion rates.
skill.summary: Improve signup conversion with focused audits, field recommendations, copy guidance, and test ideas.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found only natural-language conversion optimization guidance. No executable code, command use, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain ordinary marketing and UX terms such as description, desktop, redesign, design, and descriptive. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing routine, key handling, or executable code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose headings or bullets about signup flow optimization. There is no code path where weak cryptography could be invoked.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe product signup flow types and password strength meter UX. They do not request host inspection, environment discovery, shell commands, file reads, or system metadata collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a CRO checklist for account creation fields. No reconnaissance intent or operational mechanism is present.

content.value_statement: Signup friction lowers account creation and trial activation rates. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code audit signup flows and recommend practical conversion improvements.
actual_capabilities: Audits signup and registration flows for friction, uncertainty, and unnecessary required fields.
Recommends field order, copy, validation behavior, and post-submit experience improvements.
Suggests different patterns for B2B SaaS, B2C apps, waitlists, and e-commerce account creation.
Creates experiment ideas for layout, authentication options, messaging, and trial commitment.
Identifies measurement needs such as completion rate, field drop-off, error rate, and device differences.
limitations: It does not run analytics queries or inspect live product data by itself.
It cannot guarantee conversion lift without testing and traffic quality review.
It does not replace legal review for consent, privacy, age, or regulated industry requirements.
It focuses on signup and account creation, not full onboarding after account creation.

use_cases.title: Reduce Trial Signup Drop-Off
use_cases.description: Review a SaaS trial form and identify fields, copy, and verification steps that may prevent completion.

use_cases.title: Improve Mobile Registration UX
use_cases.description: Assess mobile signup usability, including touch targets, input types, autofill, validation, and single-column layout.

use_cases.title: Plan Signup Flow Experiments
use_cases.description: Turn observed friction into prioritized A/B test ideas for authentication, form structure, CTA copy, and trust elements.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Signup Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing signup flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this signup flow for conversion friction. The flow has these steps, required fields, and error states: [describe flow]. Return issues, impact, fixes, and priority.

prompt_templates.title: Field Reduction Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Decide which fields belong in signup versus onboarding
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this signup field list: [fields]. Classify each field as essential, optional, or deferrable. Explain the conversion impact and propose a leaner signup flow.

prompt_templates.title: Mobile Signup Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this mobile signup experience: [screens or description]. Recommend improvements for layout, keyboards, autofill, validation, CTA placement, and error recovery.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Experiment Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Prioritize CRO tests for a product team
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a signup flow experiment roadmap from this baseline data: [completion rate, drop-off points, traffic source, audience, constraints]. Include hypotheses, variants, metrics, and priority.

output_examples.input: A B2B trial signup asks for email, password, full name, company, role, team size, phone, and a required email verification before access.
output: High priority: Defer phone, role, and team size until onboarding because they are not needed to create an account.
High priority: Let users enter the product before email verification when risk allows it.
Medium priority: Add Google and Microsoft sign-in above the email form for B2B users.
Test idea: Compare email plus password against email-only first step followed by account setup.

output_examples.input: A consumer app gets many mobile signup starts but users abandon after password errors.
output: Show password requirements before input and update them as the user types.
Allow paste and add a visible password toggle.
Use clear error text that identifies the failed rule.
Test passwordless signup or Apple and Google authentication on mobile.

output_examples.input: A waitlist page asks for email, name, company, job title, budget, timeline, and a long use case answer.
output: Use email as the only required field for initial waitlist entry.
Move segmentation questions to an optional second step after submission.
Replace the long use case answer with one optional multiple-choice question.
Measure start rate, completion rate, and optional question completion separately.

best_practices: Provide current field list, step count, completion rate, and known drop-off points before asking for recommendations.
Separate compliance requirements from convenience fields so recommendations do not remove required checks.
Ask for prioritized quick wins and test hypotheses when implementation resources are limited.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to optimize a signup flow without describing the audience, product type, or required fields.
Do not treat recommendations as proven conversion lift without running controlled tests.
Do not use it for non-account lead capture forms when a dedicated form optimization skill is more appropriate.

faq.question: What signup flows does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports free trials, freemium accounts, paid account creation, waitlists, B2B SaaS, B2C apps, and e-commerce accounts.

faq.question: Can it redesign a signup form?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend field sets, field order, labels, placeholders, button copy, error copy, and layout guidance.

faq.question: Does it analyze real analytics data?
faq.answer: It can interpret analytics you provide, but it does not connect to analytics tools or collect data itself.

faq.question: Can it help with A/B test planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can turn signup friction points into hypotheses, variants, success metrics, and priority recommendations.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated products?
faq.answer: It can suggest UX improvements, but compliance requirements should be reviewed by qualified legal or compliance teams.

faq.question: How is this different from onboarding optimization?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on account creation and trial activation before the user reaches the main onboarding experience.
